description:
## Goal

Download and normalize raw source texts into clean plaintext, one file per text, with provenance metadata recorded.

## Architecture

A registry-driven acquisition pipeline. `sources/manifest.toml` enumerates every source (URL, license, format, translator). `acquire.py` reads the manifest and dispatches each entry to a format-specific downloader in `scripts/downloaders/`. Output lands in `raw/{tradition}/{text_id}.txt` paired with a `.meta.toml` provenance file (source URL, download timestamp, SHA256 of content).

## Outputs

- `sources/manifest.toml` — registry of all v1 sources
- `raw/{tradition}/{text_id}.txt` — cleaned plaintext, UTF-8 normalized, no HTML artifacts
- `raw/{tradition}/{text_id}.meta.toml` — provenance metadata

## Acceptance Criteria

- Every source in the manifest produces a corresponding file in `raw/`
- All HTML chrome (nav, scripts, styling, ads) is stripped
- Plaintext is UTF-8 normalized with no mojibake
- Provenance metadata (URL, timestamp, hash) is complete for every file
- `acquire.py` is idempotent — repeat runs skip files whose content hash already matches

## See Also

docs/guru-implementation.md §"Stage 1: Corpus Acquisition"
